q: Biodiversity comes from two words; Bio means ___.
Diversity means ___.
The term ‘Biodiversity’ was coined by ___ in 1985.
The term ‘Biodiversity’ was popularized by ___ who is now known as ‘Father of Biodiversity’.
Biodiversity refers to the variety and variability among living organisms and the ___ in which they occur.
Biodiversity can be defined as the totality of genes, species and ___ of a region.
___ can be defined as the degree of variation of life forms in an ecosystem.
___ diversity is usually taken to include the variation in ecosystems or habitats.
The number of species in a community is known as ___.
Species diversity not only considers richness, but also includes a measure of the ___ of the abundance of the different species.
Ecosystems with high species diversity tend to be more ___ than ones with limited diversity.
___ diversity refers to the variety of genetic information contained in all of the individual plants, animals, fungi and microorganisms.
Genetic diversity is the variety of ___ within a species.
Genetic diversity is important in providing populations with the ability to adapt to changes in ___ and abiotic factors.
All organisms in an ecosystem live in ___ sharing living space, shelter and available food.
Rich biodiversity allows large-scale ecosystems to function and ___.
Each species in an ecosystem has a specific ___ which is a role to play.
Healthy ecosystems will support a larger number of plant species and, therefore, a greater variety of ___.
Healthy ecosystems promote soil ___ and protection.
Healthy ecosystems and rich biodiversity will provide nutrient ___ and recycling.
Healthy ecosystems aid in ___ down pollutants.
Rich biodiversity contributes to ___ stability.
Healthy ecosystems speed ___ from natural disasters.
Healthy ecosystems provide more medicinal resources and ___ drugs.
